title: About the Household Income and Living Survey | Stats NZ
content:
## About the Household Income and Living Survey
21 June 2024, 5:45pm
This survey is currently in progress. If your household has been chosen, please contact our Information Centre , call 0800 809 464 or email hils@stats.govt.nz to arrange a time for a Survey Interviewer to meet with you.
This page has information about the Household Income and Living Survey (HILS) such as, what the information collected is used for and how often the survey is done, what your survey answers are used for, how to complete the survey, what you will be asked and who needs to take part.
- What the survey answers will be used for How to know if your household has been chosen to take the survey The type of questions asked in the survey Who in your household needs to take part How long the survey interview will take to complete How households are chosen to take part How often the survey happens
- What the survey answers will be used for
- How to know if your household has been chosen to take the survey
- The type of questions asked in the survey
- Who in your household needs to take part
- How long the survey interview will take to complete
- How households are chosen to take part
- How often the survey happens

## What the survey answers will be used for
Data from the survey is used in a range of ways by many organisations, government agencies, and charities, to improve the every-day lives of New Zealanders.
Uses for HILS data include informing the setting of important government policies, informing the positions of advocacy groups, and revealing topics of significant interest to the New Zealand public. Statistics from surveys like this feed into the consumer price index (CPI), which measures the country’s official inflation rate. The inflation rate is used, among other things, to adjust the benefit and New Zealand Superannuation rates.
The answers from this survey, once anonymised and integrated, are also used to better understand how children in New Zealand live, their homes and housing, and wellbeing so that decision makers have the right information to effect meaningful change.
Household income and housing-cost statistics: Year ended June 2025 has more information about the latest results from the survey.

## How to know if your household has been chosen to take the survey
We’ll be in touch! We will send everyone chosen for the survey a letter asking you to call our Information Centre to set up a time for one of our Survey Interviewers to visit you. Our friendly team at the Information Centre will ask for your reference code and address that will be on the letter we sent you, so make sure you have that handy. If you do not call us to make an appointment a Survey Interviewer may stop by your house to talk to you about completing the survey and make the appointment.

## The type of questions asked in the survey
The HILS mainly asks questions about household living costs, income, savings, expenditure and investments. It also asks questions on the demographics of individuals in the households and their well-being.
This year we are running the expenditure module. Which means that for a small group of survey participants there is an expenditure diary to fill in. This expenditure diary will be provided, either printed or electronic, so participants can report all purchases made over a 7 day period.
The survey questions are largely multi-choice and asked face-to-face with our staff, using a laptop to fill in your answers. There is one self-complete module, which the interviewer will ask you to fill out in privacy on their laptop; this is to ensure you feel comfortable answering personal questions.

## Who in your household needs to take part
The questions included in this survey are being asked under the Data and Statistics Act 2022, so everyone living in the household aged 15 or older needs to take part. Our approach to survey compliance has more information about your legal obligation to participate in the Household Income and Living Survey.
If your household moves away from this address, we will not contact you at your new address. You will be replaced by the new occupants of the selected address.

## How long the survey interview will take to complete
The survey interview generally takes about 20 minutes for each person. The total length of this survey varies depending on the size and make up of your household.
For those in the additional expenditure group there will be a diary provided, either printed or electronic, to fill out reporting all purchases made over a 7 day period.

## How households are chosen to take part
We use a statistical selection method where every household in our country has a chance of being included to participate in the survey. Each year thousands of households around Aotearoa New Zealand will be selected for Stats NZ’s surveys. Each of those households help represent different people and communities around Aotearoa New Zealand. The number of households helps us create a clear picture of how New Zealanders live across the country.

## How often the survey happens
HILS is an annual survey that runs from July to June each year. While the survey measures household income and housing costs every year, we collect wealth and detailed expenditure data from a smaller sample every three years.
